text.title: LLM Decision Framework — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `llm-decision-framework` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Score prompting baseline BEFORE considering RAG or FT. No baseline = no decision.
rule.rationale: Prompting closes 60-80% of the quality gap at 1% of the cost; skipping baseline biases toward expensive options.

rule.statement: If data freshness is daily / hourly, default to RAG; only consider FT for stable knowledge.
rule.rationale: FT can't keep up with daily updates; RAG refreshes by re-indexing.

rule.statement: Cap one-off $ at 5% of feature ARR; recurring at 30% of feature ARR.
rule.rationale: Spend that exceeds these ratios destroys feature P&L.

rule.statement: Sub-200ms interactive latency target → prompt-only with small model; RAG/FT add latency.
rule.rationale: RAG adds 100-300ms; FT inference is similar to base; budget accordingly.

rule.statement: Pick option the team can ship + maintain; under-skilled choice creates dead code.
rule.rationale: Buying tech the team can't operate creates an ongoing tax.

rule.statement: ADR lists rejected alternatives + reason; never just the chosen path.
rule.rationale: Without alternatives, the decision is unauditable and unreversible.
